thread.title: Kathy Hochul is 'the new Joe Biden,' NY Democrat says
thread.title_full: Kathy Hochul is 'the new Joe Biden,' NY Democrat says

author: myfox28columbus.com
title: Kathy Hochul is 'the new Joe Biden,' NY Democrat says
text: {p}Rep. Ritchie Torres, D-N.Y., on Friday took to X to condemn New York Governor Kathy Hochul as \"the new Joe Biden.”{/p}{p}Hochul, he wrote, appears to be in denial of her “vulnerabilities as a Democratic nominee.\" The remark likely refers to President Joe Biden’s decision to run for reelection despite his relative unpopularity.{/p}{p}“A Democratic incumbent who is less popular in New York than Donald Trump is in grave danger of losing to a Republican in 2026–an outcome not seen in 30 years,” he wrote.{/p}{p}Rep. Torres also seemingly referred to Biden’s eleventh-hour decision to exit the 2024 presidential race as the leading cause of President-elect Donald Trump’s victory. A similar situation in New York, he said, could lead to the state having a Republican governor.{/p}{p}“Let’s avoid repeating history and avoid sleepwalking toward impending disaster and defeat,” Rep. Torres wrote. “’Those who fail to learn from history are doomed to repeat it.’”{/p}{p}His comments come ahead of New York’s 2026 gubernatorial election, which will see Hochul campaign for another term in Albany. Rep. Torres has repeatedly expressed interest in running for governor, though he has not formally announced or launched a campaign.{/p}{p}“I will confess that I’m hardly an expert on every county in New York,” Rep. Torres told Politico Friday ahead of a planned statewide tour. “But I look forward to traveling throughout the state. I hope to embark on a listening tour and find out about the needs of New York state.”{/p}{p}Rep. Torres condemned what he called a \"leadership crisis\" in New York state in a separate social media post Thursday.{/p}{p}“Why did Donald Trump make greater gains in New York than he did elsewhere in America?” he asked via X. “The reasons might have something to do with the poverty of governance in New York.”{/p}{p}Gov. Hochul, however, attributed President-elect Trump’s gains in the state to “anti-American” and “anti-woman” sentiments among voters. She made the comment while talking to Al Sharpton, host of MSNBC’s “PoliticsNation,” about Trump’s October rally in New York.{/p}{p}“It gave me a chance to tie the Republicans running in these seats – the incumbents – closer to Donald Trump and remind everybody if you’re voting for these Republicans in New York, you are voting for someone who supports Donald Trump, and you’re anti-woman, you’re anti-abortion and, basically, you’re anti-American,\" she said.{/p}{p}{em}{a href=\"https://twitter.com/_jlwalker_\" target=\"_blank\" title=\"https://twitter.com/_jlwalker_\"}Follow {/a}Jackson Walker on X at @_jlwalker_ for the latest trending national news. Have a news tip? Send it to jacwalker@sbgtv.com.{/em}
